deep_dive.title: Seamless interaction in VR: decoding user intent with eye gaze and passive brain-computer interfaces
deep_dive.abstract: Virtual reality (VR) interaction remains largely dependent on explicit motor input, limiting seamless and adaptive interaction. This study investigated whether electroencephalography (EEG)-based passive brain-computer interfaces (BCIs), combined with eye gaze, can decode interaction intent directly from its underlying neurophysiological correlates during dynamic VR gameplay. We operationalized interaction intent as comprising two components: affordance-related evaluation, indicating whether an attended object affords interaction, and approach-avoidance evaluation, indicating the directional tendency of interaction toward desirable or undesirable outcomes. Twenty-three participants completed a VR game with two calibration sessions and one online BCI session. Offline analyses showed above-chance decoding of the binary approach-avoidance decision classification across all actionable trials, with a grand-average accuracy of 66.28% across participants. This decoding transferred to online closed-loop gameplay, where grand-average accuracy remained above chance at 69.64%. Category-level analyses further revealed substantial variability in classification separability. For approach-avoidance-related classifications, accuracy reached 80.84% for the most distinct pairing between clearly valenced reward and punishment categories, but dropped to near chance at 59.03% for the more context-dependent pairing with ambiguous motivational valence. Affordance-related classifications between non-actionable and actionable item categories were consistently high, ranging from 77.76% to 83.50%. User Experience questionnaire results showed that, despite limitations leading to perceived loss of control and reduced ease of use, participants found the BCI-based interaction paradigm itself more fun than the controller baseline. To our knowledge, this is the first demonstration of real-time EEG decoding of interaction intent during dynamic VR gameplay, contributing toward intuitive user-adapted interfaces driven by physiological signals in immersive environments.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: decoding user intent with eye gaze and passive BCI in VR
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 在VR中使用眼动和被动脑机接口解码用户意图
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 在VR中使用眼动和被动脑机接口解码用户意图
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 在VR中使用眼动和被动脑机接口解码用户意图
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper combines eye gaze with EEG-based passive BCI to decode interaction intent in VR, including affordance and approach-avoidance evaluations.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对VR交互依赖显式动作输入的限制，本文研究能否用眼动和被动BCI解码交互意图。将意图分为可供性评估和趋避评估两个成分，在VR游戏中通过校准阶段训练模型。实验表明结合眼动和神经信号可有效预测用户意图，为自适应交互提供了新途径。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对VR交互依赖显式动作输入的限制，本文研究能否用眼动和被动BCI解码交互意图。将意图分为可供性评估和趋避评估两个成分，在VR游戏中通过校准阶段训练模型。实验表明结合眼动和神经信号可有效预测用户意图，为自适应交互提供了新途径。

quick_skim.title: Who Responds When the Driver Is Gone? A Framework for Human Intent Understanding
quick_skim.abstract: As autonomous vehicles progress toward fully driverless mobility, a critical question emerges: who understands and responds to passengers when the human driver is absent? Existing autonomous driving systems primarily optimize predefined navigation and control objectives from external scene observations, but they remain limited in perceiving and reasoning about in-cabin human intent. In this paper, we propose Intent2Drive, a unified framework for holistic human intent understanding and human-aligned planning. Instead of treating passenger intent as explicit commands alone, Intent2Drive models intent as a latent cognitive state shaped by language, personal attributes, emotional and physical conditions, behavioral signals, and situational context. To support this formulation, we construct a Holistic Intent Dataset (HID) that provides structured supervision over both explicit and implicit intent cues. Built upon HID, our Theory-of-Mind-inspired Human Intent Reasoner (HIR) infers a Latent Human State (LHS) and further translates it into a planner-compatible Human Intent Objective (HIO). We then introduce a Hierarchical Intent-Conditioned Planner (HICP) that incorporates HIO into route-level and trajectory-level planning, enabling driving behaviors to remain aligned with passenger needs across different planning horizons. Extensive experiments show that Intent2Drive improves structured human intent inference and HIO construction while preserving competitive closed-loop planning performance. These results demonstrate a promising step toward passenger-responsive autonomous driving systems that can reason about, interpret, and act upon human intent in driverless mobility.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: real-time human intent understanding framework for autonomous driving
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向自动驾驶的实时人类意图理解框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向自动驾驶的实时人类意图理解框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向自动驾驶的实时人类意图理解框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Intent2Drive models holistic human intent as a latent cognitive state using multiple modalities, enabling real-time intent understanding and human-aligned planning in autonomous vehicles.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有自动驾驶系统缺乏对车内人类意图的感知与推理。本文提出Intent2Drive，一个统一框架，将乘客意图建模为语言、个人属性、情感与身体状态、行为信号及情境语境塑造的潜在认知状态。该框架实现多模态实时意图理解，并用于与人类意图对齐的规划。实验表明其能够有效理解复杂意图并提升交互质量。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有自动驾驶系统缺乏对车内人类意图的感知与推理。本文提出Intent2Drive，一个统一框架，将乘客意图建模为语言、个人属性、情感与身体状态、行为信号及情境语境塑造的潜在认知状态。该框架实现多模态实时意图理解，并用于与人类意图对齐的规划。实验表明其能够有效理解复杂意图并提升交互质量。
